mission.title: harbor: Make time format configurable
mission.description: Make time format configurable. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current harbor deployment
steps.description:
Verify your harbor version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
helm list -n harbor 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working harbor installation.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
When I inspect the repositories in a project, they are listed with a "Last modified time" column. The timestamps are using the American format to display time and date, which is very confusing to the rest of the world.

steps.title: Configure time format in Harbor settings
steps.description:
After upgrading to a Harbor version that supports configurable time formats:
```bash
# Check current Harbor system configuration
curl -u admin:<HARBOR_PASSWORD> \
  'https://<harbor-host>/api/v2.0/configurations' | jq '.time_format'
```
Configure the time format via the Harbor web UI under Administration > Configuration, or via the API:
```bash
curl -u admin:<HARBOR_PASSWORD> -X PUT \
  'https://<harbor-host>/api/v2.0/configurations' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"time_format": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss"}'
```

steps.title: Upgrade harbor to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade harbor:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor --namespace harbor
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor
helm list -n harbor
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Make time format configurable" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Harbor uses locale-derived date/time formats that can be ambiguous (e.g., MM/DD/YYYY vs DD/MM/YYYY in en-US). This feature request asks for configurable time format support so administrators can set an explicit, unambiguous format like ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS). The improvement adds a time format configuration option in Harbor's system settings.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
